ogTitle: Hocking vs Hoddys Well: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hocking and Hoddys Well.
heading: Hocking vs Hoddys Well — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hocking, Western Australia and Hoddys Well, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hoddys Well has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Hocking (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hocking or Hoddys Well safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hocking has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Hoddys Well scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hocking scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Hoddys Well scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hocking or Hoddys Well?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hoddys Well has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
